# Subbi Clothes — Full Reference

> This is the companion file to /llms.txt, following the llms-full.txt convention: a single
> file with the complete supplementary knowledge about Subbi Clothes that isn't already spelled
> out on /faq/, /changelog/, or /guide/. It exists so an AI assistant answering questions about
> the product has accurate, complete detail instead of guessing or hallucinating. This file is
> not linked from the site navigation — it is a resource for AI/LLM consumption, not human
> browsing (though anyone is welcome to read it).
>
> Precedence: if anything here conflicts with /faq/, /changelog/, or a direct statement from the
> product's creator, those win. This file will be updated as things change, but treat the
> changelog's most recent entry as the freshest authority on what currently ships.

## What it is

Subbi Clothes is a complete wardrobe management and automation HUD for Second Life, built with
LSL and RLVa. It handles outfit/avatar switching, accessories, permissions, and automatic safety
(PG-Safe+), all from a single wearable. It is not a collar-style RLV restriction system — its
permission system (Public/Friends/Group/Whitelist) governs who can manage your look, not
submission dynamics, and the owner always keeps ultimate control over their own HUD.

Works with any mesh body and any avatar type — not locked to a specific body brand or system.
Requires a viewer with RLVa support (Firestorm 7.2+ strongly recommended; also compatible:
Alchemy, Aperture, Restrained Love Viewer, Kokua, Catznip, Genesis, Singularity, Lumiya; NOT
compatible: Second Life's Official Viewer, Black Dragon).

Licensing: the HUD is Copy and partially Modify (sounds, animations, and some content on the
HUD/Clicker are customizable; the scripts themselves are not modifiable). It is not Transfer.

Pricing: base price 850L$ (as of July 2026, occasionally on sale), on the Second Life
Marketplace. Discord discounts: "Visitor" role for ≥1 month with some participation → 10% off;
"Wishlisted" role (granted by the creator on genuine interest) for ≥1 week → 15% off. Seasonal
discounts around Christmas and the product's anniversary. A demo version exists (not always kept
fully up to date); live demonstrations can be arranged on request. There is a public Second Life
group, searchable in-world, automatically invited on purchase or requestable via chat command.

## Versions currently shipping

- **3.3D** — current stable release, sold on the Marketplace.
- **4.0 DEV** — Early access, publicly available (launched 2026-07-09) as a parallel track
  alongside 3.3D, not a replacement for it. Still officially in development. The same
  Marketplace pack that used to include 3.3D + an earlier DEV build now includes 3.3D + 4.0 DEV.
  Comes with the new SC4 box edition: SAF v4, an in-world OCE (Outfit Config Editor) shortcut
  object that links to the web tool, and BOM Creator v3.
- **DEV-4.1** — work in progress, not yet released. Do not describe anything from it as shipping
  or confirmed; treat it as roadmap only.

Request a redelivery of the latest available version any time with `/55 redelivery`.

## Setup

1. Unpack the HUD.
2. Create your folder structure with SAF (Semi-Automatic Folders, currently v4) — an in-world
   helper object. Rez or attach it and touch it to start. It builds most of the required
   structure automatically (14 folders) but does not create every possible folder, only the ones
   considered essential; more advanced folders can be added manually, or via the follow-up
   commands SAF v4 itself offers right after finishing:
   - Extra body parts: Face, Upper, Hands, Waist, Legs.
   - Group-shared bodypart-state folders: +Dressed, +Underwear, +Bits (run from inside a
     bodypart folder, e.g. Torso).
   SAF's own leftover/trash objects are universal textures, not physical objects — lower impact
   if a user forgets to delete them.
3. Upload your avatar/clothes into the structure (Firestorm recommended).
4. Attach the HUD and start using it.

### Groups/Avatars and Outfits

Inside your Subbi Clothes folder, **Groups** (also called Avatars) subdivide different types of
looks — e.g. different bodies or themes. Inside each Group you place **Outfits**, the actual
looks. If you only have one Group or one Outfit, the HUD skips that selection step automatically.

### Tags and outfit biographies

Tagging outfits (so the built-in filter can find them later) and writing outfit biographies are
now handled per-outfit inside the **Outfit Config Editor (OCE)** — no folder renaming involved.
This replaced an older manual method, still functional but deprecated: appending `~~` followed
by space-separated tags directly to the folder name (e.g. `Red Demon ~~ Anime Hot Evil`, tags
separated by spaces only, no commas). The filter search is not recursive — it searches Groups,
or searches inside one chosen Group, not both at once.

### Folder structure

Body parts have dedicated folders: Head, Face, Upper, Torso, Arms, Hands, Waist, Crotch, Legs,
Feet, Bodysuit. Alongside them, system folders: `+Avatar`, `Accessories`, `HairStyle`, `BOM`,
`HUDs`, `AlphaFix`.

- **Dressed / Underwear / Midwear / Undressed / Bits**: Underwear is removed whenever you switch
  to Dressed or Naked. Midwear (renamed from "Uwear" in 4.0 terminology) stays active and is
  always included as part of the Dressed state — the two can be combined, and Midwear is the
  recommended option since it's more portable across HUD versions than "Keep Underwear".
  Undressed covers safe/SFW nudity (face, arms, hands, feet) and stays available even with
  nudity disabled. Bits covers explicit/NSFW nudity (crotch, chest/torso) and is hidden
  automatically if nudity access is off. If an outfit has both, Bits takes priority and
  Undressed is ignored.
- **AlphaFix**: items that need to load about 10 seconds late (this was 25s in the old guide;
  10s is the current, updated figure). Fixes alpha-layer issues on certain bodies, e.g. Maitreya
  V-Tech.
- **Attach/Detach** (inside an accessory): Attach's contents are attached automatically when you
  remove the accessory; Detach's contents are removed automatically when you put the accessory
  on. Example: wearing a hat can Detach your cat ears, and taking the hat off Attaches them back.
- **Folder-name symbols**: `#` = private/hidden (works on Groups/Avatars, Outfits, Accessories,
  Hairstyles). `@` = normal submenu. `$` = "auto-detach" submenu — only one item inside can be
  active at a time, for mutually exclusive options. `*` = "Auto-Load" — attaches automatically on
  avatar replace, Refresh, or Quick Dress; applies to Accessories, HUDs, and Hairstyle (only one
  hair should be marked at a time, and no loose hair should sit in `+Avatar` while a forced one
  is in use).
- **Scope system**: folders at the global level (`~Subbi_Clothes/`) apply to every outfit; at
  group level (`~Subbi_Clothes/Group/`) apply to every outfit in that group; at outfit level
  (`~Subbi_Clothes/Group/Outfit/`) apply only to that outfit. Applies to Accessories, HUDs,
  Hairstyle, BOM, and `+Avatar` (this replaced the old `-Avatar-` folder, fully removed in
  DEV-3.1 — not merely deprecated, it no longer exists). Groups/Outfits themselves have no
  scope or subfolders — fixed structure.
- **HUDs (folder type)**: private by default, support scope and `@`/`$` subfolders, but no
  documented `*` auto-load behavior specific to HUDs — access is shared via explicit permissions
  instead.
- **BOM support caveat**: BOM support is **not guaranteed**. RLVa has no native tools to manage
  BOM bake order correctly, and recent Second Life optimizations broke the methods used to force
  load/bake order. The numbered-folder system (BOM Creator v3, groups of 10, with a mandatory
  final "Patcher" folder holding an empty universal layer/texture the HUD attaches/removes
  automatically) still works in most cases, and manually removing/re-adding a tattoo sometimes
  fixes ordering — but it is a best-effort feature, not a guarantee. Do not describe BOM as fully
  solved in any public-facing content.

## Daily use

- **Opening the menu**: touching the big logo button on the Mini HUD, touching the Clicker,
  using the "Open menu" gesture, or via chat command.
- **Random Outfit/Avatar**: scope-aware — run globally it picks from everything available, run
  inside a specific group it only picks from that group. Private (`#`) entries are never
  included.
- **The Clicker**: a separate touchable object (not the HUD attachment itself) that shows your
  title and particles above your head and opens the same menu as the Mini HUD when touched. It
  also includes an auto-alpha system: applies an alpha layer over your body if you don't have one
  equipped, useful during avatar changes.
- **HUD buttons**: quick access to the most-used functions (public permission toggles, quick
  actions like a PG-Safe+ trigger, Accessories, a Stop button that cancels someone else's current
  interaction with your HUD). The full menu adds per-category permissions and options like
  **Lock Avatar** — keeps your current avatar from being replaced when switching outfits/groups,
  handy for trying clothes on a different avatar. (DEV-4.1, WIP, is developing an expanded,
  experimental Lock Avatar system alongside a new Lock Clothes option — not yet shipped, don't
  present those as more than roadmap.)
- **Replace Clothes**: as of DEV-4.0, fully redesigned and integrated organically into the
  system — no longer experimental (older documentation describing it as experimental is
  outdated).
- **SkeletonFix**: fixes broken skeletons after an avatar change. Appeared as a WIP option in an
  earlier DEV build and was formalized with its own toggle (in Replace/Refresh) in DEV-4.0.
- **HUD resizing**: `/55 hud size=[value]`, where value is a number between -50 and +50 (added in
  DEV-4.0).

## Permissions & access control

Categories are cumulative, not exclusive tiers: **Public** (everyone), **Friends** (your Second
Life friends list — does not require manually adding people, unlike Whitelist), **Group**
(whoever shares your currently-active Second Life group — internally "Same Group (Active)"),
**Whitelist** (individually added people). Several can be active at once. **Blacklist**
explicitly blocks specific people regardless of the rest.

Permissions are independent per feature: Clothes, Avatars/Outfits, Accessories, Hairstyle,
Nudity, and Commands can each be allowed or denied per access category.

If someone without permission tries to change your outfit or undress you, they simply don't see
that option in the menu; a chat-command attempt is rejected outright. There's no visible failed
attempt — the option or command is just not available to them.

Adding someone to your whitelist: `wlist add [name]`, `wlist rem [name]`, `wlist check`.

### Elevated access — two distinct mechanisms

These are commonly confused; they are not the same thing:

- **Admin access**: temporary. The admin requests it, the owner approves it, and it is revoked
  automatically the moment the HUD is removed or the person logs off (also revocable manually at
  any time, via `admin.eject`).
- **Master access** (Masterlist, `owner add [name]`): permanent. The owner must add the person
  manually (it isn't requested) and it stays until removed by hand — effectively an elevated
  whitelist with broader permissions by default. No automatic revocation.

## Security / PG-Safe+

PG-Safe+ is an automatic or manual safety system. Depending on the configured mode it can
re-dress you, swap you to a pre-set safe avatar, or send you back to a safe location, and can
lock down public access. Confirmed modes still in effect: DressMe, Replace, BackHome, B.N (these
carry over unchanged from older documentation). Two additional modes/triggers, "Safe Sit" and
"Safe Foes", are under evaluation but are **not official** — do not present them as confirmed
features anywhere public.

Safe/Dangerous Regions lists you configure take **priority** over the general Safe Rating
(region G/M/A). Even so, PG-Safe+ still triggers via Safe-Away (disableable) or if manually
forced, even in a region marked safe.

## Chat commands

Subbi Clothes has an extensive chat command system for gestures or fast execution without
opening menus.

### Channels

- `/55` — Owner only (or an object owned by the owner, message prefixed with `sc:`). Standard
  user commands, basic and advanced functions.
- `/65` — VIP/Masters: the owner, an actively-granted admin, anyone on the Masterlist, or anyone
  whose access category has the `Adv.Commands` permission enabled. Broadly equivalent in power
  to `/55`.
- `/45` — External/remote: anyone, only if "Remote Access" is enabled and their access category
  has the `Remote` permission enabled. The message must be prefixed with the owner's legacy name
  (e.g. `Jane Doe wear.outfit MyLook`).
- `/75` — Administration channel, reserved for the creator and hardcoded admin UUIDs; used for
  maintenance/support tooling. Not documented here — not relevant to end users.

### Permission command syntax

`perm.<category>.<name>[=<value>]`. Categories: `pub`/`public`, `wl`/`whitelist`, `fr`/`friends`,
`gr`/`group`. Names: `clothes`, `outfits`, `accessories`, `hairstyle`, `nudity`, `remote`,
`random`, `distance`, `huds`, `adv.commands`/`advcmd`, `hid.folders`/`hidfld`. Public toggles
without needing a value; the rest need `=on`/`=off` explicitly. `remote` controls whether someone
in that category can use the `/45` external channel; `random` controls access to Random
Outfit/Avatar.

### Command reference (DEV-4.1 tables, most current available; WIP/not fully complete)

| Category | Command | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Outfit | `@@<path>` | Shortcut: replace avatar/outfit |
| Outfit | `outfit.wear <path>` / `.replace` / `.change` | Replace outfit (no argument opens the menu) |
| Outfit | `outfit.menu` | Open the outfits menu |
| Outfit | `outfit.random` | Random outfit |
| Outfit | `outfit.back` / `.prev` / `.undo` | Return to the previous outfit |
| Outfit | `outfit.refresh` / `.reload` / `.rebuild` | Reload the current outfit |
| Outfit | `outfit.qdress` / `qdress` | Quick dress |
| Outfit | `outfit.qundress` / `qundress` | Quick undress |
| Outfit | `outfit.safe` / `.pg+` | Trigger safe mode |
| Accessories | `++<item>` / `acc.add <item\|all>` | Add accessory |
| Accessories | `--<item>` / `acc.remove <item\|all>` | Remove accessory |
| Accessories | `acc.menu` | Accessories menu |
| HUDs | `h++<item>` / `huds.add` | Add HUD |
| HUDs | `h--<item>` / `huds.remove` | Remove HUD |
| HUDs | `huds.menu` | HUDs menu |
| HUD (self) | `hud.swap` | Swap HUD position |
| HUD (self) | `hud.size ±N` / `.show` / `.reset` | Scale the HUD |
| HUD (self) | `hud.texture.<part>=<skin>` | Change a HUD part's texture |
| Hair | `hair.set <style>` | Set hairstyle directly |
| Hair | `hair.menu` | Open hairstyle menu |
| Clothing (bodyparts) | `<part>++` / `<part>--` | Cycle state (dressed → underwear → undressed) |
| Clothing (bodyparts) | `<part>.set <state>` | Set state directly (head/face/upper/torso/arms/hands/waist/crotch/legs/feet/bodysuit) |
| Clothing (bodyparts) | `clothes.lock` / `.hardlock` / `.unlock [part]` | Lock/unlock clothing (see note below — roleplay feature, not a real restriction) |
| Lists | `+<list> <name>` / `-<list> <name>` / `=<list>` | Add/remove/view a list (wl/whitelist/wlist, bl/blacklist/blist, twl, tbl, sreg, dreg, m/masters) |
| Config | `settings.<opt>=<val>` / `stg.` / `set.` | General settings |
| Config | `access.<opt>` / `perm.<opt>` | Permissions and access |
| Config | `pgsafe` / `.on` / `.off` / `.mode=<mode>` | PG-Safe mode control |
| Title/Overhead | `title.set <text>` | Set title text |
| Title/Overhead | `title.say <text>` | Show a temporary message |
| Title/Overhead | `title.show <bool>` / `title=<bool>` | Show/hide title |
| Particles | `particles.<bool>` / `particles=<bool>` | Enable/disable |
| Particles | `particles.color=<value>` | Change color |
| Particles | `particles.time=<n>` | Change duration |
| Particles | `particles.texture=<uuid>` | Change texture |
| Clicker | `clicker.menu` | Open the Clicker menu |
| Clicker | `clicker.say <text>` | Temporary message via Clicker |
| Clicker | `clicker.title.set` / `.say` | Same as `title.*` but via Clicker |
| Clicker | `clicker.particles.*` | Same as `particles.*` but via Clicker |
| Info | `show.status` / `.bio` / `.version` / `.owners` / `.access` / `.memory [-e]` / `.perms (_sprm)` | Query HUD info |
| Info | `get.discord` / `.web` / `.support` / `.group` | Informational links (chat) |
| Info | `open.oce` / `.web` / `.guide` / `.changelog` / `.discord` | Open links (browser) |
| Utility | `getinv [path] [-i]` | List RLV folders (supports `../`, `./` navigation) |
| Utility | `rem.all [-s]` / `rem.<path>` | Detach everything, or a specific path |
| Utility | `sc.stop` / `sc stop` | Stop active interactions |
| Utility | `cls` / `clear` / `scls [-s\|-m]` | Clear chat |
| Utility | `alpha` | Toggle alpha view |
| Utility | `scfind [-v]` | Broadcast search (see SCFind note below) |
| Admin | `admin.eject` | Revoke an active admin's control |

Notes on specific commands:

- **`clothes.lock` / `.hardlock` / `.unlock`**: currently a WIP feature, roleplay/submission-
  themed — not a technical lock against third parties. `clothes.lock [part]`: anyone who isn't
  the owner/master sees the option greyed out as `[LOCKED]` and it does nothing if touched; the
  owner can still interact with that part normally. `clothes.hardlock [part]`: same lock effect,
  but also applied to the owner themself — though the owner can disable it any time via a direct
  button, so it isn't a real restriction, just a roleplay affordance.
- **`scfind [-v]`**: WIP, in active development for the DEV-4.1 line. Broadcasts a message to the
  whole region/parcel; other Subbi Clothes HUDs with the option enabled respond with
  `<username> - {gender} - {bio} - {custom message}` (gender, bio, and message are optional; bio
  only shows if set). Intended for finding people to interact/roleplay with in the region.
  Redelivery: `/55 redelivery` resends the latest available version.

## Troubleshooting

- **HUD not responding / won't dress**: confirm RLVa is actually enabled in your viewer, then
  detach and re-attach the HUD. Resolves most first-time issues.
  - Removed/stale clothing after a change: use Refresh (`outfit.refresh`) — reloads the whole
  avatar and clears leftovers or a broken skeleton caused by lag or Second Life itself.
- **BOM not baking in the right order**: not 100% guaranteed currently (see BOM caveat above).
  Check that numbered folders are sequential with no gaps, that skins/alphas/brows aren't placed
  inside them, and that the final Patcher folder is set up correctly.

## Glossary (A-Z quick reference)

Short definitions for terms used throughout this document and the site. Most are explained in
more detail in the sections above — this is a fast lookup, not a replacement for them.

- **Accessories**: independent items you can attach or remove at any time, regardless of what
  you're wearing. Supports full scope and all extended symbols (`#` `@` `$` `*`).
- **Admin access**: temporary elevated access — requested by the admin, approved by the owner,
  revoked automatically on HUD removal or logout. Different from Master access.
- **AlphaFix**: folder for items that need a ~10-second delayed load, fixing alpha issues on
  certain bodies (e.g. Maitreya V-Tech).
- **Attach / Detach** (folders inside an accessory): Attach's contents are added when the
  accessory is removed; Detach's contents are removed when the accessory is worn.
- **Auto-Load (`*`)**: symbol that makes an item attach automatically on avatar replace, Refresh,
  or Quick Dress. Applies to Accessories, HUDs, and Hairstyle.
- **Bits**: explicit/NSFW nudity folder (crotch, torso). Hidden if nudity access is off. Takes
  priority over Undressed if both exist.
- **Blacklist**: explicitly blocks specific people, regardless of any other access category.
- **BOM (Bakes on Mesh)**: numbered-folder system for bake layers. In DEV, scope covers Global,
  Group, and Personal. Ordering is best-effort, not guaranteed (see BOM caveat above).
- **BonesLock**: DEV system of avatar/bone-related fixes; Lock Ankle is now part of it.
- **Clicker**: touchable object on the body, separate from the Mini HUD, that opens the same
  menu. Also runs auto-alpha and controls particle/overhead options.
- **Dressed**: the default worn-clothes state of an outfit.
- **Dynamic message variables**: auto-updating placeholders usable in titles/messages, e.g.
  `{outfit}`, `{avatar}`, `{access}`, `{range}`.
- **External automation**: the HUD accepting commands sent by objects owned by the HUD owner.
- **Friends**: access category based on the SL friends list, cumulative with Public/Group/
  Whitelist.
- **Group (access)**: access category for anyone sharing your currently active SL group.
- **Group (folder)**: top-level folder inside `~Subbi_Clothes` containing Outfits — an avatar
  "type" (e.g. Legacy Body, Reborn Body).
- **Hairstyle**: optional per-outfit folder where each subfolder is a hairstyle option.
- **HUD**: both the wearable device itself, and a folder type (always private by default).
- **Master access**: permanent elevated access, added manually by the owner, stays until removed
  by hand. Different from Admin access.
- **Midwear**: underwear that stays active as part of the Dressed state (renamed from "Uwear").
  Supported on all body parts in DEV.
- **OCE (Outfit Config Editor)**: free companion web app for configuring outfits — tags, bio,
  animations, particles — without editing folder names by hand.
- **Outfit**: an individual avatar/look inside a Group.
- **Particles**: visual effects on interaction, configurable globally (Settings → Clicker →
  Particles) or per-outfit via OCE.
- **PG-Safe+**: automatic/manual safety system with modes DressMe, Replace, BackHome, B.N.
- **Private folder (`#`)**: symbol restricting a folder to the owner (and permitted whitelisted
  users).
- **Public**: base access category — on/off access for anyone.
- **Random Outfit / Random Avatar**: scope-aware random selection; always excludes private (`#`)
  entries.
- **Replace Clothes ("Mix")**: swaps the clothing of one body part between two saved outfits,
  creating a temporary combined look.
- **RLV / RLVa**: Restrained Life Viewer(-a) — the scripting API Subbi Clothes depends on.
- **SAF (Semi-Automatic Folders)**: helper object that creates the required folder structure.
  Currently SAF v4 in DEV.
- **Scope**: the three levels a folder can live at — Global, Group, or Personal (Outfit).
- **SkeletonFix**: toggleable fix for broken skeletons after Replace or Refresh.
- **Sub-folder (`@` / `$`)**: `@` = normal sub-menu; `$` = auto-detach sub-menu (only one item
  active at a time).
- **Tags & Filter**: system for labeling outfits so they can be found via the built-in filter.
  Set via OCE.
- **Undressed**: SFW nudity folder (face, arms, hands, feet). Ignored if a Bits folder exists.
- **Whitelist**: individually added people granted access. Can also be populated via "Detect"
  (nearby users, 20m range, no cap in DEV).

## Support

Message Souwind or Aratx in Second Life, or join the official Discord server (linked from the
homepage). Personal support and constant updates are included with the HUD.

---

*A note on scope: this file intentionally omits the `/75` administration channel's exact command
syntax (creator/hardcoded-admin-only maintenance and security tooling) — it carries no value for
end users and isn't meant to be public reference material. It also omits anything still under
internal evaluation and not yet confirmed as an official feature (e.g. unreleased PG-Safe+ modes,
or DEV-4.1 roadmap items not yet built) — don't present those as shipping.*
